articles.headline: RBI board approves record ₹2.11 lakh crore dividend for FY24
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India approved a record surplus transfer of ₹2,10,874 crore to the Central Government for the 2023-24 fiscal year. This is a 141% increase over the previous year's dividend of ₹87,416 crore. The windfall is significantly higher than the government's budgeted estimate of ₹1.02 lakh crore, providing a major boost to the fiscal position.

articles.headline: Nifty and Sensex hit all-time highs on RBI dividend boost
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices reached record highs following the RBI's announcement of a higher-than-expected dividend payout. The Nifty 50 surged 1.6% to close at 22,597, while the Sensex gained 1,196 points to end at 74,221. Banking and financial services stocks led the market rally, as investors cheered the potential for reduced government borrowing.

articles.headline: IndiGo reports ₹1,895 crore Q4 profit on robust travel demand
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation, the operator of IndiGo, reported a net profit of ₹1,894.8 crore for the quarter ended March 2024, a 106% year-on-year increase. The airline's revenue rose 26% to ₹17,821 crore. Management also announced plans to launch a premium business class product on its domestic routes by late 2024 to cater to increasing corporate travel demand.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to hear telcos' plea on AGR dues calculation errors
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to list a petition from major telecom operators including Vodafone Idea and Bharti Airtel. The petition seeks to rectify alleged mathematical errors in the calculation of their Adjusted Gross Revenue (AGR) dues. A favorable outcome could provide significant financial relief to the sector, which currently owes billions in statutory dues.

articles.headline: IMD issues red alert as severe heatwaves hit Northern India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department issued a red alert for Delhi, Rajasthan, Punjab, and Haryana as temperatures crossed 47 degrees Celsius. Rajasthan's Sirsa recorded a peak of 47.8 degrees. Authorities warned of high risks of heat-related illnesses and advised citizens to avoid outdoor activities. The extreme conditions are expected to persist for the next four days.

articles.headline: Delhi government requests additional water from Haryana amid crisis
articles.summary: Water Minister Atishi stated that the Delhi government has written to Haryana seeking the release of more water into the Yamuna. The capital is facing a severe shortage as water levels at treatment plants have dropped. The crisis has been exacerbated by the ongoing heatwave, which has driven up demand while reducing raw water supply from neighboring states.

articles.headline: Adani Energy Solutions board approves ₹12,500 crore fundraise
articles.summary: The board of Adani Energy Solutions has approved a proposal to raise up to ₹12,500 crore through the issuance of shares to institutional investors. The fundraise aims to support the company’s expansion in smart metering and power transmission projects. This move follows the group's efforts to increase capital expenditure across its infrastructure and utility businesses.

articles.headline: Awfis Space Solutions IPO subscribed 2.02 times on opening day
articles.summary: The initial public offering of co-working space provider Awfis Space Solutions saw healthy demand, closing Day 1 with 2.02 times subscription. Retail investors showed the most interest, subscribing 6.02 times their allotted portion. The company aims to raise ₹599 crore to fund its expansion plans and meet general corporate expenses.

articles.headline: SEBI simplifies registration process for Foreign Portfolio Investors
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has introduced new measures to ease the entry of global capital into Indian markets. The changes include allowing digital signatures for FPI registrations and streamlining document requirements. These reforms are intended to improve the 'ease of doing business' for international funds investing in India's equity and debt markets.

articles.headline: UK inflation drops to 2.3% in April, nearing 2% target
articles.summary: The UK's annual inflation rate fell to 2.3% in April, down from 3.2% in March, reaching its lowest level in nearly three years. The decline was largely driven by lower domestic energy prices. This cooling of inflation increases the likelihood that the Bank of England will consider interest rate cuts in the summer to support the broader economy.

articles.headline: Global oil prices fall on US interest rate hike concerns
articles.summary: Brent crude prices declined toward $82 per barrel as US Federal Reserve officials signaled that interest rates may stay higher for longer. Higher rates often slow economic activity and reduce demand for fuel. Markets are also awaiting the OPEC+ meeting on June 2, where members will decide whether to extend current production cuts into the second half of the year.

articles.headline: Microsoft unveils AI-focused 'Copilot+' PCs with specialized hardware
articles.summary: Microsoft introduced a new category of Windows PCs called 'Copilot+' that feature dedicated chips for artificial intelligence tasks. These devices allow for on-device AI processing, offering features like 'Recall' to track user activity. The hardware partners include Dell, Lenovo, and HP, marking a significant shift toward local AI integration in consumer electronics.

articles.headline: KKR beats SRH to secure spot in IPL 2024 final
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders became the first team to enter the IPL 2024 final after a comprehensive eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad in Qualifier 1. Mitchell Starc took 3 wickets for 34 runs, while Shreyas Iyer and Venkatesh Iyer both scored unbeaten half-centuries. SRH will get another chance to reach the final when they play the winner of the Eliminator.

articles.headline: CEA Urges Indian Corporates to Increase Domestic Capital Investment
articles.summary: Chief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran has called on the Indian corporate sector to reinvest its profits into the domestic economy rather than building cash reserves. He noted that while the average profitability of listed companies has risen to 30%, there is a noticeable reluctance to increase on-ground investment despite a stable post-pandemic regulatory environment.

articles.headline: EV Adoption in India Varies Significantly Across Different States
articles.summary: Electric vehicle adoption remains uneven across India, driven by diverse state-level tax breaks and incentives. Uttar Pradesh leads the market with over 600,000 EV sales, primarily in the three-wheeler segment. Meanwhile, states like Karnataka and Maharashtra are seeing higher penetration in the two-wheeler market due to registration fee exemptions and better infrastructure.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Facility Selected for Advanced Fighter Jet Production
articles.summary: The Indian government has approved the allocation of 600 acres in Puttaparthi, Andhra Pradesh, for a new facility to manufacture the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA). The ₹1 lakh crore project involves the DRDO and private partners. The facility aims to produce 140 fifth-generation stealth fighters, with the first prototype expected to take flight by 2027.

articles.headline: Investors in Talks for 1.6 Billion Dollar Rajasthan Royals Buyout
articles.summary: A consortium led by the Samani family is in advanced negotiations for a $1.63 billion buyout of the IPL franchise Rajasthan Royals. The deal involves purchasing stakes from existing shareholders including RedBird Capital and Manoj Badale. The move requires regulatory approval from the BCCI and highlights the soaring valuations of Indian Premier League teams.

articles.headline: Private B-School Placement Packages Increase Amid Strong Corporate Demand
articles.summary: Top private business schools in India, including XLRI and SPJIMR, report a significant rise in average salary packages for the 2026 batch. Average offers have swelled to nearly ₹33 lakh per annum as consulting and financial firms ramp up hiring. The trend reflects a recovery in the white-collar job market despite global macroeconomic uncertainties.

articles.headline: Oscars to Maintain Human Creativity Requirement Amid Growing AI Use
articles.summary: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has clarified that while AI tools can be used in filmmaking, only human creators will be eligible for Oscar nominations. New rules require studios to disclose AI usage, but the Academy emphasized that human creativity must remain at the center of the cinematic arts to protect the integrity of the awards.

articles.headline: EC Assures Supreme Court of Compliance in Bengal Election Counting
articles.summary: The Election Commission has informed the Supreme Court that it will strictly adhere to established rules for counting votes and supervising polling stations in West Bengal. The assurance came during a hearing on a plea by the Trinamool Congress, which sought specific central oversight to ensure transparency and prevent irregularities during the final phase of polling.

articles.headline: West Bengal Polls See High 87 Percent Turnout in Final Phase
articles.summary: The final phase of the West Bengal assembly elections concluded with a massive 87% voter turnout across key constituencies. While some reports of minor scuffles emerged from South 24 Parganas, the polling was largely peaceful. The high turnout has led both the ruling TMC and the opposition BJP to claim confidence in securing a majority.

articles.headline: Punjab Government Wins Trust Vote Amid Opposition Boycott and Protests
articles.summary: The Bhagwant Mann-led AAP government in Punjab successfully passed a confidence motion in the state assembly with 88 votes. The session was boycotted by opposition parties who accused the government of misuse of power. The trust vote followed allegations by AAP that the BJP was attempting to topple the state government through 'Operation Lotus'.

articles.headline: Himanta Biswa Sarma Likely to Retain Assam CM Post if BJP Wins
articles.summary: Internal reports suggest that the BJP leadership is leaning towards retaining Himanta Biswa Sarma as the Chief Minister of Assam if the party returns to power. Exit polls have predicted a strong showing for the NDA. Party leaders in Delhi are reportedly finalising the cabinet structure as they prepare for the official results on counting day.

articles.headline: Congress Instructs Assam Workers to Ignore Exit Polls and Stay Alert
articles.summary: The Congress party has directed its workers in Assam to remain vigilant at counting centers and ignore exit poll predictions that favor the BJP. State in-charge Jitendra Singh claimed the party's internal surveys show the Congress-led alliance winning between 70 to 75 seats, contradicting mainstream media forecasts of an NDA landslide.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Reconstitutes Financial Committees for 2026-27 Term
articles.summary: Speaker Om Birla has reconstituted four major parliamentary standing committees, including the Public Accounts Committee and the Committee on Public Undertakings. While most chairpersons have been retained, the reshuffle aims to enhance oversight of government spending and ministry performance for the upcoming fiscal year ending April 2027.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar Shifts Residence to New Bungalow Near Lalu Prasad
articles.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar has moved into a new official residence at 7, Circular Road in Patna, located just 100 meters from the residence of RJD leader Lalu Prasad Yadav. The move has sparked political speculation regarding a potential thaw in relations between the former allies, though both parties officially describe the shift as a routine administrative matter.

articles.headline: Manipur Remains Tense Three Years After Outbreak of Ethnic Violence
articles.summary: Three years since the start of ethnic conflict in Manipur, the state continues to face sporadic violence and deep social divisions despite the restoration of a popular government. Over 58,000 people remain displaced in relief camps, and while central forces have stabilized major towns, rural areas continue to report incidents of firing and unrest between different communities.

articles.headline: Nepal's New Prime Minister to Prioritize Domestic Issues Over Foreign Travel
articles.summary: The newly appointed Prime Minister of Nepal, Balen Shah, has indicated that he will refrain from making any foreign trips during his first year in office. Shah intends to focus exclusively on domestic economic reforms and infrastructure development. This departure from tradition aims to address the country's immediate internal challenges before engaging in high-level diplomacy.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panels Flag Major Budgetary Gaps in Central Ministries
articles.summary: Standing committees have expressed concern over significant unspent funds in several central ministries, including Defence and Housing. Reports indicate that funds are often cut during the Revised Estimate stage because departments are unable to utilize their initial allocations effectively. The panels have called for better financial planning and faster project implementation.

articles.headline: NCLT Approves Mantra Properties’ Bid for Distressed Mulund Project
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved a ₹215 crore resolution plan by Mantra Properties to acquire a stalled residential project in Mulund, Mumbai. The project, originally belonging to Nirmal Lifestyle, has liabilities exceeding ₹1,491 crore. The buyout is expected to provide relief to over 235 homebuyers who have been waiting for their apartments for years.

articles.headline: Government Allows 100 Percent FDI in Insurance via Automatic Route
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry has notified new rules allowing 100% Foreign Direct Investment in the insurance sector through the automatic route. This move eliminates the need for prior government approval for foreign investors, aiming to attract more global capital. However, the 20% FDI cap for Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) remains unchanged under the new regulations.

articles.headline: Revised FEMA Rules Tighten Oversight on Companies with Chinese Stakes
articles.summary: The government has amended the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) rules to mandate prior approval for any investment in Indian firms where a Chinese entity holds more than 10% stake. The move is designed to prevent 'opportunistic takeovers' and enhance national security oversight over foreign investments coming from countries that share a land border with India.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry Urges Debt Recovery Tribunals to Speed Up Case Disposal
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry has held high-level talks with Debt Recovery Tribunals (DRTs) to address the growing backlog of cases. Officials emphasized the need for faster resolution of high-value defaults to help banks recover bad loans. The ministry has proposed the use of more Lok Adalats and digital hearings to streamline the recovery process and improve the health of the banking sector.

articles.headline: High Court Rules in Favor of GSK in Paxil Trademark Dispute
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has upheld GlaxoSmithKline's (GSK) trademark rights for the antidepressant 'Paxil' against Shreya Life Sciences. The court ruled that Shreya's use of a similar mark constituted infringement, despite the defendant's claim that GSK had not used the brand name in India for several years. The judgment reinforces the protection of global pharmaceutical trademarks.

articles.headline: France Pushes for Clear Framework in India-EU Free Trade Talks
articles.summary: France has called for a more 'enforceable and granular' framework in the ongoing India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations. During a visit to New Delhi, French officials emphasized that a win-win deal must include concrete commitments on non-tariff barriers and sustainable development. The negotiations, which resumed recently, aim to significantly boost bilateral trade in services and goods.

articles.headline: Concorde Group Signs Major Office Lease Deal with BHIVE in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Real estate developer Concorde has leased 1.4 lakh square feet of Grade-A office space in Bengaluru to co-working firm BHIVE Workspace. The 15-year agreement is one of the largest flexible office space transactions in the city this year. The deal reflects the growing demand for managed office solutions as companies continue to adopt hybrid work models.

articles.headline: The Psychology and Speculation Behind Indian Election Exit Poll Results
articles.summary: An analytical piece explores why Indian voters and politicians remain obsessed with exit polls despite their frequent inaccuracies. The article discusses the 'science' of poll-making and the emotional impact these predictions have on party workers. It highlights how exit polls often serve as a form of 'political entertainment' before the actual results are declared.

articles.headline: AI in the Family: From Digital Assistant to Protector and Playmate
articles.summary: This opinion piece explores the evolving role of Artificial Intelligence within the modern household. Moving beyond simple tasks, AI is increasingly being viewed as a 'family member' that offers protection and companionship. The author discusses the emotional connection people are forming with AI and the potential social implications of this technological integration.

articles.headline: Tamil Superstar Vijay's Political Move Sparks Debate in South India
articles.summary: The article discusses the political ambitions of Tamil actor Vijay and the potential impact of his new party on the upcoming elections. It analyzes his carefully crafted public image and his efforts to position himself as a credible alternative to established political dynasties. The move is seen as a significant shift in the intersection of cinema and politics in Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: New Zealand Minister Apologizes After Controversial Butter Chicken Remarks
articles.summary: A New Zealand minister has issued an apology following comments about the origins of 'Butter Chicken' that were deemed offensive by the Indian community. The remarks sparked a debate on social media about cultural appropriation and the history of the popular dish. The incident highlights the sensitivities surrounding culinary heritage in a globalized world.

articles.headline: Great Indian Bustard Conservation Efforts Show Signs of Success
articles.summary: Conservationists report that the first batch of Great Indian Bustard chicks hatched through an artificial breeding program are thriving in Rajasthan. The project aims to save the critically endangered species from extinction. Experts believe that these efforts, combined with habitat protection, offer a glimmer of hope for the survival of one of the world's heaviest flying birds.

articles.headline: The Rise of Female Operatives in India's Organized Crime Networks
articles.summary: A deep dive into the growing involvement of young, educated women in North India's criminal gangs. These women often use social media to recruit members and manage operations under the radar of law enforcement. The report examines how 'gangster girls' are breaking traditional gender roles within crime syndicates, often acting as honey-traps or strategic coordinators.

articles.headline: Is the Global AI Investment Boom Reaching a Sustainability Breaking Point?
articles.summary: In the AI world, major tech companies are increasingly investing in each other, creating a circular economy that some analysts find concerning. The article explores whether the massive valuations of AI startups are sustainable or if the industry is heading towards a bubble. It questions if the current 'boom' is driven by genuine technological utility or purely by speculative capital.

articles.headline: Fountain Pens Witness Resurgence Among Gen Z and Young Millennials
articles.summary: The traditional fountain pen is experiencing a comeback as younger generations seek 'analogue' alternatives to digital fatigue. Sales of high-end and artisanal pens have surged, with many users citing the tactile experience and environmental sustainability as key reasons for the switch. The trend has also boosted the community of pen collectors and calligraphy enthusiasts globally.

articles.headline: Donald Trump Expresses Reluctance for Further Military Strikes on Iran
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump has stated that while he wants to reach a diplomatic deal with Iran, he has no immediate desire to resume military hostilities. Speaking at a campaign rally, Trump claimed his administration's 'maximum pressure' policy had brought Tehran to the negotiating table. He warned, however, that the US remains ready to act if its interests are threatened.

articles.headline: US to Withdraw 5,000 Troops from Germany Amid NATO Strategy Shift
articles.summary: The United States has announced plans to withdraw 5,000 troops from its bases in Germany as part of a broader realignment of its forces in Europe. NATO officials are reportedly working to understand the implications of this move for regional security. The decision comes amid ongoing debates over defense spending and the future of the trans-Atlantic alliance.

articles.headline: Spirit Airlines to Cease All Operations After 34 Years in Business
articles.summary: Ultra-low-cost carrier Spirit Airlines is set to wind up its operations following a period of severe financial distress and a failed merger attempt. The Florida-based airline, known for its budget-friendly fares, will leave a significant gap in the US domestic aviation market. The closure will affect thousands of employees and millions of passengers who relied on its low-cost model.

articles.headline: Proposed US Auto Duties Could Cost German Economy 18 Billion Dollars
articles.summary: A new report suggests that proposed US tariffs on imported vehicles could lead to an $18 billion loss for the German automotive industry. The hike would significantly impact major manufacturers like Volkswagen and BMW, who rely heavily on exports to the American market. Economists warn that such trade barriers could trigger a wider slowdown in the European economy.

articles.headline: India Finalizes Squad for T20 World Cup Amid Balance Concerns
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team has announced its squad for the upcoming T20 World Cup, sticking with a familiar core of experienced players. While the inclusion of veterans provides stability, some analysts have raised concerns about the lack of young power-hitters and the overall balance of the bowling attack. The team is under pressure to end its long drought in ICC tournaments.

articles.headline: How Formula One Drivers Train Their Necks to Withstand G-Forces
articles.summary: F1 drivers undergo intense physical training to prepare their neck muscles for the extreme G-forces experienced during high-speed cornering. Using specialized weights and resistance bands, they build the strength needed to keep their heads stable at speeds over 300 km/h. This physical conditioning is crucial for maintaining focus and preventing serious injuries on the track.

articles.headline: Carlos Alcaraz Faces Injury Setback Ahead of Major Clay Court Season
articles.summary: Tennis star Carlos Alcaraz has been forced to withdraw from several key tournaments due to a persistent arm injury. The setback comes just as the clay-court season peaks, casting doubt on his fitness for the upcoming French Open. Fans and analysts are concerned that the young Spaniard's aggressive playing style may be leading to frequent physical issues early in his career.

articles.headline: Saudi Arabia Defends LIV Golf Investment Despite Claims of Failure
articles.summary: Despite critics calling LIV Golf an expensive failure, Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund continues to back the league as a long-term strategic asset. Officials argue that the venture has successfully disrupted the global golf landscape and is part of a broader plan to diversify the kingdom's economy through sports and entertainment tourism.

articles.headline: International Testing Agency Reports High Rate of Doping in Olympic Sports
articles.summary: The International Testing Agency (ITA) has flagged a concerning rise in doping cases across several Olympic disciplines. The agency noted that advanced testing methods are uncovering more violations, particularly in weightlifting and athletics. The report calls for increased global cooperation and more frequent out-of-competition testing to ensure a fair playing field for all athletes.

articles.headline: Wrexham AFC's Hope for Consecutive Promotion Ends in Playoff Defeat
articles.summary: Wrexham AFC's dream of reaching the English Premier League has been put on hold after a narrow defeat in the promotion playoffs. Despite a record-breaking season and high-profile ownership by Hollywood stars, the team failed to secure the final step up. The club's management has promised to regroup and push for promotion again in the next season.

articles.headline: Pierre Hermé: The Pastry Chef Who Revolutionized the Modern Macaron
articles.summary: World-renowned pastry chef Pierre Hermé reflects on his 50-year career and how he transformed the traditional macaron into a global luxury icon. Known as the 'Picasso of Pastry', Hermé introduced bold flavor combinations like rose and lychee, moving away from classic French recipes. Today, his brand operates over 110 outlets worldwide, influencing pastry trends from Paris to Tokyo.

articles.headline: Inside the Mystery of the World's Most Valuable Unsolved Art Heist
articles.summary: The Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum heist remains unsolved 36 years after 13 masterpieces worth $500 million were stolen. Despite thousands of leads and a $10 million reward, works by Vermeer and Rembrandt are still missing. The article explores the leading theories and the ongoing efforts by the FBI to recover the lost treasures, which are considered irreplaceable cultural artifacts.

articles.headline: Radio Taiso: The Simple Exercise Routine Behind Japan's Famous Longevity
articles.summary: Every morning at 6:30 am, millions of Japanese citizens tune in to 'Radio Taiso' for a short, synchronized calisthenics routine. This century-old tradition is credited with helping maintain the health and mobility of Japan's elderly population. The low-impact exercises are performed in parks, schools, and offices, fostering a sense of community while promoting lifelong fitness.

articles.headline: Met Gala to Feature Plus-Sized Mannequins in Historic Fashion Exhibit
articles.summary: The Metropolitan Museum of Art's upcoming 'Costume Art' exhibition will include plus-sized mannequins for the first time in its history. The move aims to challenge traditional beauty standards and reflect a more inclusive view of fashion across the centuries. Curators say the exhibit will showcase how the 'dressed body' has evolved, highlighting diverse body types and disability visibility.

articles.headline: The Untold Story of the Cooks Who Fed Chernobyl's Workers
articles.summary: Forty years after the Chernobyl disaster, a new historical account sheds light on the women who worked in the plant's canteen during the cleanup. Despite extreme radiation levels, these cooks provided meals for the 'liquidators' who were struggling to contain the nuclear meltdown. Their story highlights the often-overlooked human courage and the long-term health toll on the local population.

articles.headline: City judge dead in suspected suicide at Green Park residence
articles.summary: A 30-year-old judge of the Karkardooma city court was found dead in the bathroom of his flat in South Delhi. Relatives of the deceased suggested the judicial officer took the extreme step due to ongoing domestic disputes with his wife and sister-in-law. Police have not yet issued an official statement regarding the incident.

articles.headline: Iran says ball in US court, ready for talks or war
articles.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister Kazem Gharibabadi stated that the U.S. must choose between diplomacy and confrontation as peace talks remain frozen. President Donald Trump has rejected a proposal to end the U.S. blockade and open the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran maintains it is prepared for both a negotiated settlement and continued conflict to secure its interests.

articles.headline: SC dismisses TMC plea after Election Commission’s assurances
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a petition by the Trinamool Congress regarding the appointment of counting personnel in West Bengal. The decision followed assurances from the Election Commission that state government representatives would be part of the process. The court ruled that the current circular providing for a mix of central and state employees is lawful.

articles.headline: Election Commission orders repeat polls in Bengal’s Falta seat
articles.summary: The Election Commission has ordered fresh polling at all 285 stations in the Falta constituency for May 21. The move follows reports of the subversion of the democratic process and evidence of EVM tampering during the second phase of voting on April 29. Repolling was also conducted at 15 booths in other constituencies on Saturday, recording high turnouts.

articles.headline: Four personnel killed in IED explosion during Chhattisgarh demining
articles.summary: Four security personnel, including a District Reserve Guard inspector, were killed when an improvised explosive device exploded in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district. The incident occurred while a police team was attempting to neutralise the device in a forested area near the Narayanpur border. Deputy CM Vijay Sharma described the loss as tragic and promised government support.

articles.headline: Delhi government approves Metro Phase 5B expansion plan
articles.summary: The Delhi government has approved a Rs 48,204.56 crore expansion of the Metro rail network. The Phase 5(B) project will add 97km of new lines and 65 stations across seven corridors. The expansion aims to connect outer localities like Najafgarh, Narela, and Mithapur with central Delhi by a 2029 target date, pending final Union Cabinet approval.

articles.headline: Manipur inquiry panel yet to submit findings after three years
articles.summary: A three-member Commission of Inquiry probing the 2023 Manipur ethnic clashes has missed four deadlines to submit its report. The panel, set up in June 2023, has received its latest extension until May 2026. Official sources indicate that key stakeholders, including top police and government officials from the time of the initial violence, have not yet been summoned.

articles.headline: Jabalpur boat capsize toll rises to 11 as search continues
articles.summary: The death toll from the Narmada Queen boat capsize in Bargi Dam rose to 11 after divers recovered two more bodies, including a six-year-old child. The vessel's helmsman stated the incident was caused by a sudden storm. Families have begun repatriating the remains of victims to Delhi and Tamil Nadu, while three individuals remain missing following the tragedy.

articles.headline: Protests in Pune after 65-year-old man kills minor girl
articles.summary: A 65-year-old labourer was arrested in Pune for the alleged sexual assault and murder of a three-and-a-half-year-old girl. The incident sparked massive public protests and road blockades. Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis promised the case will be heard in a fast-track court and stated the government would seek the death penalty for the accused.

articles.headline: Man kills wife and four children in Gurugram village
articles.summary: A 39-year-old man allegedly poisoned and killed his wife and four children before attempting suicide in Wazirpur village, Gurugram. The children were aged between 8 and 14 years. The suspect is currently in critical condition at a government hospital. Preliminary investigations suggest the motive may be linked to domestic issues, though the man will be questioned upon recovery.

articles.headline: Indian IT firms oppose US plan to curb offshore call centres
articles.summary: Indian IT companies and industry bodies like NASSCOM are preparing to challenge a proposed US Federal Communications Commission rule. The rule seeks to cap offshore call volumes for American companies and mandate language proficiency disclosures. Indian firms argue the move, framed as a security measure, could severely impact revenue and service quality in the outsourcing sector.

articles.headline: Oscars overhaul international film rules and set AI restrictions
articles.summary: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has updated its rules to allow films winning top prizes at major festivals like Cannes to bypass traditional country submissions for the international film category. Additionally, the Academy ruled that only roles demonstrably performed by humans will be eligible for awards, addressing concerns regarding the use of generative AI.

articles.headline: Tirumala Ghee Adulteration: Panel holds former TTD officials responsible
articles.summary: A committee probing ghee procurement for Tirumala laddus between 2019 and 2024 has blamed former TTD executive officer AV Dharma Reddy for major lapses. The report alleges a systemic breakdown and the weakening of tender norms allowed 7 million kilograms of adulterated ghee into the system. The panel recommended blacklisting erring suppliers and disciplinary action against officials.

articles.headline: Three-tier security deployed for Tamil Nadu election counting
articles.summary: The Election Commission has implemented a three-tier security system at 62 counting centres in Tamil Nadu. Around 20,000 police personnel and Central Armed Police Forces will secure the perimeters, entry gates, and strongrooms. Use of mobile phones is restricted, and QR code-based ID cards have been introduced to prevent unauthorised entry during the process on May 4.

articles.headline: Police inspector suspended after rape accusation in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: Inspector Chinna Mallaiah of the Guntur Range has been suspended and is currently absconding after being accused of raping a 40-year-old tenant. Medical examinations confirmed the assault, and two special teams have been formed to apprehend him. A case has been registered under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita following the victim's complaint on Wednesday.

articles.headline: Man arrested in Kozhikode after 25 years on the run
articles.summary: Kozhikode police have arrested a man identified as Hameed, who had been absconding for nearly 25 years following the alleged murder of his wife in Edachery. The arrest is part of a recent initiative by the station to clear long-pending cases. The suspect was traced after the probe was relaunched, more than two decades after the September 2001 incident.

articles.headline: Palakkad hoteliers protest against Rs 1,000 commercial LPG hike
articles.summary: Hotel and restaurant owners in Palakkad held a protest march against a recent hike in commercial gas cylinder prices. Protesters stated that the sudden increase makes sustaining business difficult. The Kerala Hotel and Restaurant Association has also announced a statewide strike on May 6 to demand a rollback of the price increase, which they label as unfair.

articles.headline: Karnataka assembly disqualifies Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni after conviction
articles.summary: The Karnataka Legislative Assembly has officially disqualified MLA Vinay Kulkarni following his life imprisonment sentence in a 2016 murder case. The disqualification is in accordance with the Constitution and the Representation of the People Act. Kulkarni will remain disqualified for six years after his release unless the conviction is stayed by a competent court.

articles.headline: Kerala DGP announces tight security for assembly vote counting
articles.summary: DGP Ravada A Chandrasekhar confirmed that 25 companies of central forces and the entire state police force have been mobilised for Kerala's vote counting on May 4. Special arrangements are in place for politically sensitive areas prone to post-poll violence. CCTV and drone surveillance have been enhanced to ensure the process remains peaceful and secure.

articles.headline: Karnataka transport workers announce indefinite bus strike from May 20
articles.summary: Unions representing four state-run transport corporations in Karnataka have called for a statewide indefinite strike starting May 20. The workers are demanding a 25% salary hike effective from January 2024. The joint action committee stated the strike will involve staff from KSRTC, BMTC, NWKRTC, and KKRTC unless their demands are addressed by the government.

articles.headline: Telangana CM promises to clear Rs 6,000 crore retirement dues
articles.summary: Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy announced that the Telangana government will disburse Rs 6,000 crore in pending retirement benefits within 100 days. He also directed the Pay Revision Commission to submit its report immediately for salary revision decisions. Additionally, health cards will be issued to all government employees by June 1 following a meeting with employee unions.

articles.headline: West Bengal repolls record 86.9% turnout across 15 stations
articles.summary: Repolling in 15 stations within the Magrahat Paschim and Diamond Harbour constituencies of West Bengal concluded with an average turnout of 86.9%. The Election Commission ordered the fresh polls following allegations of EVM tampering during the second phase. Officials described the process as peaceful, with security forces maintaining a high presence throughout the day.

articles.headline: Mamata predicts TMC victory and dismisses exit poll results
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee alleged that exit polls predicting a BJP lead in West Bengal are a 'scam' intended to manipulate the stock market. During a virtual meeting with party agents, she predicted the TMC would win more than 200 seats. She also instructed agents to demand recounts in any stations where the margin of lead is narrow.

articles.headline: BJP accuses AAP of political vendetta over Sandeep Pathak FIRs
articles.summary: The Punjab BJP has accused the AAP government of political vendetta following reports that Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak was booked in two non-bailable cases. Pathak is one of seven MPs who recently defected from AAP to the BJP. Pathak claimed he had no official information about the FIRs, while the BJP stated it would oppose any attempts at administrative harassment.

articles.headline: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar begins nine-day visit to Caribbean nations
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar has started a visit to Jamaica, Suriname, and Trinidad and Tobago to strengthen bilateral ties and engage with the Indian diaspora. The trip aims to boost South-South cooperation and development. Jaishankar will hold discussions with regional leaders and interact with business members of the Girmitiya communities in these countries.

articles.headline: Three workers killed in Jammu bridge collapse; probe ordered
articles.summary: An old bridge in the Bantalab area of Jammu collapsed during construction work, killing three workers and injuring another. The bodies were recovered following a nightlong search and rescue operation by the Army, NDRF, and SDRF. Chief Minister Omar Abdullah expressed sorrow over the deaths and directed authorities to conduct a thorough inquiry to fix responsibility.

articles.headline: Court rejects pre-arrest bail for Nida Khan in Nashik TCS case
articles.summary: A Nashik court rejected the anticipatory bail plea of Nida Khan, a prime accused in a case involving alleged sexual harassment and forced religious conversions at a Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) unit. The prosecution argued that custodial interrogation is necessary for the investigation. Nine FIRs have been registered in the matter, and several other suspects are in police custody.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry notifies reforms allowing 100% FDI in insurance
articles.summary: The Union Finance Ministry has notified new rules allowing 100% foreign direct investment in the insurance sector through the automatic route. The notification also implements relaxed norms for companies with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India, identifying seven critical sectors including advanced battery components and rare-earth processing.

articles.headline: Four children bludgeoned to death in Uttar Pradesh village
articles.summary: In Ambedkar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, four children aged between 7 and 14 were found bludgeoned to death inside their home. Their mother, Naziya Khatoon, is missing and has been named as a suspect in an FIR registered by the children's uncle. Police suspect the children were killed with a blunt object and have formed multiple teams to trace the mother.

articles.headline: Mizoram reports fresh influx of refugees from Myanmar
articles.summary: Over 50 Myanmarese nationals have crossed into Mizoram's Zokhawthar village following aerial bombings by the Myanmar military at Khawpuichhip village. Reports indicate that seven people, including six children, were killed in the strikes across the border. Local police confirmed the influx as tensions remain high in Myanmar's Chin state.

articles.headline: Four workers die after being buried under coal slurry in Dhanbad
articles.summary: Four labourers were killed at the Moonidih coal washery in Jharkhand's Dhanbad district when a large chunk of coal slurry collapsed on them. The accident occurred while the workers were loading trucks. Local villagers and family members staged a protest following the incident, demanding compensation and jobs for the dependents of the deceased.

articles.headline: Spirit Airlines ceases operations as first Iran war industry casualty
articles.summary: U.S. budget carrier Spirit Airlines has shut down all operations after failing to secure a government bailout or creditor support. The airline's collapse is attributed to a doubling of jet fuel prices caused by the Iran war and maritime disruptions. The liquidation marks the first major aviation industry casualty of the conflict, resulting in thousands of job losses.

articles.headline: Canada intelligence report identifies Khalistani extremists as national security threat
articles.summary: The 2025 annual report by the Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) states that Khalistani extremists continue to pose a national security threat to Canada. While acknowledging that most advocacy is peaceful, the report notes that a small group uses Canada to promote or fund violence in India. It also lists India alongside China and Russia as perpetrators of foreign interference.

articles.headline: Donald Trump vows US will take over Cuba immediately
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump stated during a dinner speech in Florida that the United States would 'take over' Cuba almost immediately. He described the Cuban government as an extraordinary threat to U.S. values and national security. The comments came alongside the announcement of new sanctions by the White House, which the Cuban government has firmly rejected.

articles.headline: Trump compares US naval blockade of Iran to piracy
articles.summary: President Donald Trump described U.S. Navy operations seizing cargo and oil from ships heading to or from Iranian ports as being 'like pirates.' The maritime blockade was implemented to pressure Tehran following the failure of peace talks. Trump asserted the operations are a profitable business and warned that the U.S. will not exit the confrontation prematurely.

articles.headline: United States approves $8.6 billion arms sales to West Asian allies
articles.summary: The U.S. has bypassed congressional review to approve military sales exceeding $8.6 billion to Israel, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE. Despite these sales, Washington warned European allies of delivery delays as domestic weapon stockpiles have been drained by the ongoing war. The Pentagon maintained the blockade of Iranian ports will continue indefinitely to enforce compliance.

articles.headline: Manas Dhamne storms into maiden ATP Challenger final
articles.summary: Indian tennis talent Manas Dhamne reached his first ATP Challenger final with a straight-sets victory over France's Antoine Ghibaudo in Shymkent, Kazakhstan. The 6-4, 6-3 win on clay marks a significant milestone for the young player. Dhamne’s heroic run comes as part of his transition to the senior professional circuit.

articles.headline: CSK defeat Mumbai Indians as Hardik’s side nears playoff exit
articles.summary: Chennai Super Kings secured an eight-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians at the Chidambaram Stadium, keeping their playoff hopes alive. Ruturaj Gaikwad and Kartik Sharma scored half-centuries to chase down MI's total of 159. The defeat marks Mumbai's seventh loss in nine matches, effectively ending their chances of qualifying for the next round of the IPL.

articles.headline: Harmanpreet Kaur to lead India in T20 World Cup squad
articles.summary: The BCCI announced a 15-member squad led by Harmanpreet Kaur for the upcoming T20 World Cup and England series. The team sees the return of Radha Yadav and the inclusion of uncapped pacer Nandani Sharma following injuries to key players. India is paired with Australia and South Africa in Group 1, with the tournament kicking off against Pakistan on June 14.

articles.headline: Bull Run Sees Fewer Deep Corrections, Domestic Liquidity Cushions Markets
articles.summary: The current Indian bull market, ongoing for over 2,229 days, exhibits fewer deep corrections and shallower drawdowns compared to past cycles. Domestic institutional investor inflows of over ₹20-lakh crore have provided a strong stabilizing force, offsetting foreign outflows and absorbing high valuations.

articles.headline: Specialised Investment Funds See Rapid Growth, Attract Smaller City Investors
articles.summary: Specialised Investment Funds (SIF) from SEBI are rapidly gaining traction, with 50% participation from smaller cities, a higher proportion than general mutual funds. SIF AUM more than doubled to ₹10,620 crore in March. Fund houses rush for first-mover advantage despite distribution challenges.

articles.headline: India Eases FDI Rules for Firms with Up to 10% Chinese Stake
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry amended FEMA Rules, allowing overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese (or other land-bordering country) shareholding to invest in India via the automatic route. This aims to boost manufacturing in electronic components, capital goods, and solar cells. Majority ownership and control must remain Indian.

articles.headline: APTransco Internships to Develop AI Solutions for Power Grid
articles.summary: APTransco is launching a Summer Internship Programme 2026 for IIT and premier institute students. Interns will work on 44 problem statements, developing deployable AI models for areas like solar/wind forecasting, cybersecurity, and asset inspection, receiving a stipend of up to ₹25,000/month.

articles.headline: Commercial LPG Cylinder Prices Hiked, Causes Scramble for Domestic Gas
articles.summary: Commercial LPG cylinders increased by ₹993, prompting a rush for unchanged domestic LPG. Despite no hike in domestic prices, fear of future increases led to a scramble among consumers in New Delhi.

articles.headline: Five Corporate Governance Traps Destroying Investor Wealth Identified by SEBI
articles.summary: An analysis of SEBI orders reveals five recurring corporate governance traps that destroy investor wealth: misused funds, inflated profits, questionable asset transfers, stock price manipulation despite weak fundamentals, and manufactured price discovery. Investors must look for red flags.

articles.headline: D2C Brands Pose "Trojan Threat" to Established Indian Consumer Companies
articles.summary: Traditional Indian consumer giants like Page Industries (Jockey) and VIP Industries face disruption from D2C brands that target premium urban consumers with compelling product stories. This shift is impacting growth rates and valuations of incumbents.

articles.headline: Federal Bank Acquires Standard Chartered India Credit Card Portfolio
articles.summary: Federal Bank will acquire Standard Chartered Bank India's credit card portfolio of 4.5 lakh cards, expanding its card base and presence in top Indian cities. The move aims for seamless customer transition and deeper market penetration, benefiting from StanChart's urban customer concentration.

articles.headline: Health Insurance for Fertility Treatments Often Excluded in India
articles.summary: Most Indian health insurance policies exclude IVF and assisted reproductive treatments, classifying them as elective. Some insurers offer specialized plans or add-ons with waiting periods and sub-limits. It's crucial for consumers to verify specific coverage and plan financial buffers due to high costs.

articles.headline: Top Performing NPS Funds Across Equity, Government, and Corporate Bonds
articles.summary: Data for top NPS pension funds as of April 30 shows ICICI Prudential Pension Fund leading in 5-year CAGR for Tier I Equity (13.9%), LIC Pension Fund for Government Bonds (5.8%), and HDFC Pension Fund for Corporate Debt (6.8%).

articles.headline: Top Performing PMS Schemes by 5-Year Returns Across Market Caps
articles.summary: PMS Bazaar data as of March 31, 2026, highlights top-performing PMS schemes. Aequitas Investment Consultancy Opportunities leads Small Cap (44.6% CAGR), Tulsian PMS in Large Cap (18.5%), and Shapoorji Financial Advisors Value Magno in Multi Cap (23.7%).

articles.headline: HDFC Equity Savings Fund Upgraded to Four-Star Rating
articles.summary: HDFC Equity Savings Fund's improved risk-adjusted performance led to a four-star rating upgrade. The fund allocates equally across equity, arbitrage, and debt, making it suitable for cautious investors seeking equity taxation benefits with lower risk and average 11.5% annual returns over five years.

articles.headline: SIP Returns Hit Unevenly Amid Volatile Equity Market Conditions
articles.summary: From September 2024 to April 2026, Indian equity markets experienced volatility due to global headwinds, affecting SIP returns unevenly. Large-cap funds lagged, while mid-cap funds led, benefiting from industrials and capex-linked stocks. Retail investor flows remained robust despite corrections.

articles.headline: Caution Advised for OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO Due to High Rates, Unclear Mix
articles.summary: Digital lender OnEMI Technology Solutions (Kissht) IPO values the company at 1.4x post-issue book value. Despite robust growth, caution is advised due to average lending rates over 30%, evolving portfolio mix with new LAP offerings, and potential credit downcycle risks.

articles.headline: Global Corporations Prioritize India Growth, Scale Up Investments and Presence
articles.summary: Major global corporations like Amazon, Coca-Cola, Hilton, CNH Industrial, Crown Holdings, NOV, Sensata, AAK, Carlsberg, DWS Group, and Andritz AG are significantly expanding their presence and investments in India, citing strong growth opportunities and localized strategies.

articles.headline: "Who Am I?" Investment Quiz for Corporate India Knowledge
articles.summary: A five-clue quiz challenges investors to identify a small-cap company with over 50 years of leadership, strong shareholder returns, a highly credentialed chairman, and ongoing capacity expansion, despite single-digit institutional shareholding.

articles.headline: Nifty Shows Mild Positive Bias, Nifty Bank Signals Bearish Inclination
articles.summary: Nifty 50 derivative data indicates a mild positive bias, while Nifty Bank data points to a bearish inclination, having slipped for a second consecutive week. FIIs maintain a bearish stance. Nifty Bank is expected to moderate further before an uptrend.

articles.headline: Infosys Stock Expected for Pullback Rally, Buy 1180-Strike Call Option
articles.summary: Infosys stock is expected to stage a pullback rally despite a downtrend, with immediate resistance at ₹1,250 and ₹1,310. Traders are advised to buy the 1180-strike call option for May expiry, targeting ₹1,300 with a stop-loss at ₹32.

articles.headline: Tata Consumer Products Futures: Wait for Breakout Beyond ₹1,200
articles.summary: Tata Consumer Products stock is consolidating between ₹1,110 and ₹1,200. Traders are advised to avoid shorting now, but go long on May futures if it breaks out of ₹1,200, targeting ₹1,300 with a stop-loss at ₹1,150.

articles.headline: Titan Company Futures Expected to Resume Rally, Target ₹4,650
articles.summary: Titan Company May futures are consolidating but expected to resume their rally, breaking past ₹4,570 to reach ₹4,650. Traders can go long at ₹4,400 with a stop-loss at ₹4,290, trailing it as the price rises.

articles.headline: Choosing Between Futures and Options: Delivery Margins and Payoff
articles.summary: This article discusses factors in choosing between long futures and in-the-money (ITM) equity call options as expiry approaches. Delivery margins for futures and ITM calls increase to 100% of contract value. Futures offer symmetrical payoffs with higher potential gains but also higher loss risk.

articles.headline: Understanding F&O Ban: Market Wide Position Limit Exceeded
articles.summary: Stocks enter an F&O ban when their aggregate open interest in futures and options exceeds 95% of the Market Wide Position Limit (MWPL), which is 20% of free-float shares. New positions are penalized, and normal trading resumes when OI drops below 80% MWPL.

articles.headline: Gold Futures Expected to Trade Range-Bound Between ₹1,48,000 and ₹1,55,000
articles.summary: MCX gold futures dropped early last week but recovered, remaining below the 21-day moving average. No strong bullish reversal signs, expected to trade range-bound between ₹1,48,000 (support) and ₹1,55,000 (resistance). Silver futures show no definite trend.

articles.headline: Crude Oil Futures Breakout, Brent Range-Bound, Next Move Unclear
articles.summary: MCX crude oil futures broke above ₹9,500 to ₹10,571, while Brent crude remains in a $94-114 range. The next trend depends on Brent breaking out of this range. Traders with high risk appetite can buy MCX futures at ₹9,000, targeting ₹12,000.

articles.headline: Nifty, Sensex, and Nifty Bank Expected to Reverse Higher Soon
articles.summary: Despite FPI selling and recent declines, Indian benchmark indices (Nifty 50, Sensex, Nifty Bank) are expected to reverse higher from current support levels. Nifty could target 25,400-25,500, Sensex 81,500-82,000, and Nifty Bank 58,000-58,500 in the short term.

articles.headline: Asian Paints Shares Show Support, Advised to Hold or Accumulate on Dips
articles.summary: Asian Paints stock finds support at ₹2,100, with resistance at ₹2,600. Investors can either hold with a stop-loss at ₹2,280 and target ₹2,830, or accumulate on dips at current levels with a wider stop-loss at ₹1,990.

articles.headline: ABB Stock Bullish for Long Term, Target ₹10,000
articles.summary: ABB's long-term outlook is bullish, confirmed by a double-bottom pattern and strong trendline support. Investors can buy now and accumulate on dips at ₹6,850, with a stop-loss at ₹5,640, targeting ₹10,000 in the long term.

articles.headline: Tejas Networks Downtrend, Limited Upside to ₹500 in Short Term
articles.summary: Tejas Networks stock is in a downtrend, with strong resistance at ₹660, making a rise to ₹1,000 unlikely. Investors with risk appetite can hold with a stop-loss at ₹375, aiming for ₹500 in the short term.

articles.headline: Siemens Stock Bullish Breakout, Target ₹4,800 Over Next Year
articles.summary: Siemens stock shows a bullish breakout from a prolonged sideways range. Strong supports are at ₹3,450 and ₹3,350. Investors can buy now and accumulate on dips at ₹3,580, with a stop-loss at ₹3,180, targeting ₹4,800 over the next year.

articles.headline: Ajanta Pharma Retains Bullish Outlook, Maintain Long Position
articles.summary: Ajanta Pharma stock is consolidating but retains a bullish long-term outlook above ₹2,800. Traders can retain long positions initiated at ₹2,868, with a target of ₹3,800, adjusting stop-loss as price rises.

articles.headline: US Markets Maintain Bullish Trend, Dow Jones, S&P 500, NASDAQ to Rise
articles.summary: US benchmark indices (Dow Jones, S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite) continue their broader uptrend, expected to rise further. Dow Jones could break above 50,000, NASDAQ to 26,300-26,500. Dollar index and Treasury yield show mixed outlook.

articles.headline: Bandu's Blockbusters: Weekly Stock Picking Challenge
articles.summary: A weekly stock-picking challenge where participants guess which of five selected stocks will give the best return by next Friday. The winner receives a ₹2,000 prize.

articles.headline: Indian Market Indices Show Mixed Performance Amid Sectoral Shifts
articles.summary: Nifty 50 and Sensex gained slightly last week, while BSE Teck, IT, and Oil & Gas sectors performed strongly. BSE Bankex and PSU declined. The report provides a snapshot of leading companies' fundamentals and financial performance, including P/E, P/BV, ROCE, and DER.

articles.headline: Overview of Latest Bank Fixed Deposit Interest Rates
articles.summary: A comparative table showing current fixed deposit interest rates (1-year to 5-year) for various foreign, Indian public sector, and private sector banks, including small finance banks, for amounts below ₹1 crore.

articles.headline: RBI Proposes New Safeguards to Combat Digital Payment Frauds
articles.summary: RBI's discussion paper suggests measures like a one-hour delay for account-to-account transfers above ₹10,000 to individuals and a "kill switch" for all digital payments to combat rising authorized push payment (APP) frauds. Proposals also include specific safeguards for elderly and disabled users.

articles.headline: Financial Planning for Mid-Career Job Uncertainty and Retirement
articles.summary: A financial planning case study for a 49-year-old single individual facing job uncertainty. Recommendations include laddered FDs for liquidity, redirecting FD interest to PPF, and focusing on skill upgrades to improve early retirement flexibility, aiming for a realistic corpus.

articles.headline: Mutual Fund Star Ratings Across Equity, Hybrid, Debt and Passive Categories
articles.summary: A comprehensive list of mutual fund star ratings based on historical rolling performance (returns and risk), Sortino ratio, and expense ratios. Categories include Large Cap, Mid Cap, Small Cap, Flexi Cap, Multi Cap, Value, Dividend Yield, Sectoral, Hybrid, Dynamic Asset Allocation, Debt funds, and Passive funds.

articles.headline: TVS Motor Sales Up 7% in April, Despite Supply Chain Issues
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company recorded 473,970 units in April sales, a 7% y-o-y increase. However, dispatch volumes were impacted by supply chain constraints, raw material shortages, and container availability. Electric vehicle registrations saw a robust 36% growth.

articles.headline: Crude Surge Poses Price, Not Supply Shock for India: CEA Nageswaran
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran stated that the current crude oil scenario presents a price shock, not a supply shock, for India. He cautioned that the current account deficit could widen, and fiscal deficit estimates might be challenged. He also noted potential remittance shocks from the Gulf region.

articles.headline: Adani Power Enters Nuclear Energy, Lines Up Land and SPVs
articles.summary: Adani Power, India’s largest private thermal power producer, is building an early-stage pipeline in nuclear energy, focusing on land aggregation, corporate structuring, and regulatory readiness for future policy clarity. The company is also evaluating international projects.

articles.headline: Electric 3-Wheeler Sales Up in April, Led by L5 Vehicles
articles.summary: India's electric three-wheeler sales saw modest month-on-month growth in April, reaching 61,260 units. Growth was primarily driven by L5 vehicles (higher-speed, lithium-ion powered), expanding nearly 4%, while L3 declined. Rising fuel costs are accelerating the shift towards EVs for cargo.

articles.headline: Power Ministry Targets 60% Local Content in HVDC Substations by FY35
articles.summary: The Power Ministry set an ambitious roadmap for 60% Minimum Local Content (MLC) in High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) substations by FY35, phased from 30% by FY28. This aims to promote self-sufficiency and reduce imports in capital-intensive transmission projects.

articles.headline: Swiggy Appoints Swapnil Bajpai as CEO of Dineout, Scenes Verticals
articles.summary: Swiggy elevated company veteran Swapnil Bajpai to CEO of its Dineout and Scenes businesses, aiming to scale its dining-out and experiences verticals. Bajpai will oversee restaurant discovery, bookings, bill payments, and events, marking Swiggy's expansion beyond core food delivery.

articles.headline: India's E-Bus Sales Up 19% in First Four Months of 2026
articles.summary: India's electric bus market grew 19% y-o-y in Jan-Apr 2026, with 1,868 registrations. April saw a dip after a March spike, reflecting infrastructure readiness. New entrants are beginning pilot supplies. Industry expects sales to build in H2 2026 as manufacturing ramps up.

articles.headline: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Profit Rises 18% Amid RBI Scrutiny
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank reported an 18% YoY net profit increase to ₹4,027 crore for Q4. Net interest income rose 13% to ₹8,756 crore, while bad loan provisions dropped 43%. Asset quality improved with a 1.2% NPA ratio. The bank faces ongoing RBI pressure regarding digital banking and credit cards.

articles.headline: India-bound LPG tanker safely exits Strait of Hormuz amid tensions
articles.summary: The Marshall Islands-flagged LPG carrier Sarv Shakti, carrying 45,000 tons of fuel for Indian Oil Corp, successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz. This transit is crucial for Indian energy security after disruptions due to regional tensions. The vessel is expected to reach Visakhapatnam on May 14.

articles.headline: Prasoon Joshi appointed Chairman of Prasar Bharati
articles.summary: Lyricist Prasoon Joshi, former CBFC chief, has been appointed as the new Chairman of Prasar Bharati, India's public service broadcaster. His appointment fills a vacancy since December 2025 and aims to modernize the broadcaster and expand its global reach.

articles.headline: Rohit Jain appointed RBI Deputy Governor for three years
articles.summary: Rohit Jain, an RBI Executive Director, has been appointed Deputy Governor for a three-year term, effective May 3, 2026. He will oversee departments including banking regulation and supervision, succeeding M. Rajeshwar Rao and T Rabi Sankar.

articles.headline: Niti Aayog appoints two new full-time members
articles.summary: The government appointed Dr. R. Balasubramaniam and Dr. Joram Aniya as new full-time members of Niti Aayog. Balasubramaniam specializes in public policy and healthcare, while Aniya is an agricultural economics expert. The appointments increase the total members to seven.

articles.headline: Nationwide test of Cell Broadcast System for disaster alerts successful
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications successfully tested a new indigenous Cell Broadcast System (CBS) for sending emergency alerts to mobile phones. Developed by C-DOT, the system provides loud, vibrating pop-up notifications that bypass network congestion, designed for time-sensitive disasters.

articles.headline: SAIL stock recommended for accumulation amid steel sector upcycle
articles.summary: SAIL stock is advised for accumulation on dips due to expected strong earnings growth driven by steel price recovery and cost control, supported by safeguard duties. Despite trading at a 15-year high, its price/book value suggests potential.

articles.headline: Concord Biotech stock breaks out; RBL Bank also shows breakout
articles.summary: Concord Biotech stock has broken above ₹1,100 and is recommended for accumulation with a target of ₹1,400. Separately, RBL Bank stock has broken out at ₹332 and is advised for long positions with a target of ₹400.
